events.title: Orange Light and Restless Money
events.scene: The Pacific coast is a country reinventing itself by the year — gold rushes spent, land booms rising, the rattle of prospectors giving way to boosters and railroads. The {surname} line can chase the boom, sell shovels to the dreamers, or feed and entertain the endless tide of newcomers. The coast belongs to whoever arrives unafraid.
events.researchNote: Place-specific West Coast origins beat (EX-2): the Gold Rush aftermath, land-boom speculation, and service-to-the-stampede fortunes. Generic to any west_coast founding.

choices.text: Buy land ahead of the boosters and the railroads
choices.outcome: You buy orange groves and town lots before the crowd arrives. When the railroad reaches them, the {surname} name is suddenly a coastal fortune.

choices.text: Sell shovels, lodging, and pleasure to the stampede
choices.outcome: You profit from every dreamer who passes through, whether they strike it rich or not. The {surname} line learns that the surest gold is other people's hope.

choices.text: Build the towns the coast will need when the boom settles
choices.outcome: You build for the city that comes after the rush. The {surname} name is poured into foundations the coast will stand on for a century.

events.title: Where No One Asks Where You're From
events.scene: On the coast a name is whatever you say it is, and the past stays back east where you left it. The {surname} line can build an identity from pure ambition, unburdened by the old country — or hold to the values it carried west. The frontier rewards reinvention; it does not always reward roots.
events.researchNote: The West Coast as the place of self-reinvention — a distinct cultural lever vs the rooted Old World origins. Sets identity-fluidity flags.

choices.text: Make the {surname} name mean only what you make it mean now
choices.outcome: You build the {surname} name on momentum alone. No one asks where you came from, and you never volunteer it.

choices.text: Carry the old values west and root them in new soil
choices.outcome: You keep faith with what you brought. The {surname} line is a piece of the old country transplanted, thriving in the coastal sun.
